text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there lived an old owl named Olive. Olive knew that autumn was her season. She liked the crunch beneath her feet, the orange leaves swirling around her.

One crisp moonlit night, Olive heard a strange racket coming from next door. It wasn't owlNoise. It was... music. Something was happening at the young girl's treehouse. Olive hooted softly.

The next morning, Olive peeked through curtains. There was Mia, spinning an old record with gleeful fervor. Mia seemed quite pleased with her creation.

"A tapestry!" exclaimed Mia. She pressed play. Olive couldn't believe her ears. A symphony of leaves.

Olive hooted joyously. The birds seemed to join her. A flock of sparrows chirped delightfully.

Days turned into weeks. Mia invited Olive to listen. They flew over. Mia hung her record from a branch. Every time she spun it, something magical happened.
